techcrunch.title: Thiel Capital’s Jack Selby nabs stakes in hot startups like Etched through Arizona connections
techcrunch.description: Selby's VC firm, Copper Sky Capital, is currently raising a $300 million second fund, according to a regulatory filing.

techcrunch.title: IQM, Europe’s first public quantum company, admits the future of the tech is uncertain
techcrunch.description: IQM, a full-stack quantum company out of Finland, went public on the Nasdaq today at a valuation of about $1.9 billion.

techcrunch.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
techcrunch.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

techcrunch.title: A warning sign about AI’s real cost, courtesy of Google and Amazon
techcrunch.description: AI has made it a lot harder for tech companies like Amazon and Google to deliver on their net-zero pledges.

techcrunch.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
techcrunch.description: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

techcrunch.title: Travel app Hopper to pay $35M in FTC settlement over ‘unfairly’ charging hidden fees
techcrunch.description: Hopper will pay $35 million to settle FTC allegations that it used deceptive “dark patterns” to hide fees and mislead travelers about the costs and benefits of services.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
techcrunch.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

techcrunch.title: Boeing-owned Wisk Aero accused of firing manager who raised safety concerns
techcrunch.description: A former software manager claims Wisk rushed software testing ahead of a crucial 2025 flight test.

techcrunch.title: Melinda Gates’ venture firm backs Magnify Ventures’ $46.6M Fund II
techcrunch.description: Early-stage firm Magnify Ventures has raised a $46.6 million Fund II from LPs, including Melinda French Gates’ Pivotal Ventures.

techcrunch.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
techcrunch.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

techcrunch.title: Popular TV-tracking app TV Time is shutting down as company focuses on AI
techcrunch.description: TV Time, the popular TV-tracking app, is shutting down on July 15 as parent company Whip Media pivots toward enterprise AI products.

techcrunch.title: Lucid Motors’ CFO is out as its new CEO continues leadership shakeup
techcrunch.description: The company announced a new slate of executive hires meant to help turn things around, as Gravity SUV sales are not taking off as expected.

techcrunch.title: US government says it got hacked — again
techcrunch.description: A top Democrat on the Senate's Intelligence Committee warned that the information accessed on a Homeland Security intelligence-sharing network may risk national security.

techcrunch.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
techcrunch.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

techcrunch.title: Tesla saw a massive sales jump in the second quarter
techcrunch.description: The company delivered more than 480,000 EVs globally, seemingly thanks to geographic expansion and cheaper versions of the Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck.

techcrunch.title: Rivian raises EV sales forecast as Q2 production ramps up
techcrunch.description: The company now expects to ship a few thousand more vehicles by the end of 2026 than it previously expected, after launching its R2 SUV last month.

techcrunch.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
techcrunch.description: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

techcrunch.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
techcrunch.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

techcrunch.title: Apple is reportedly planning new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro releases early next year
techcrunch.description: Apple is readying several new iPad Pro tablets and a budget-friendly MacBook Pro, reports suggest.

techcrunch.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
techcrunch.description: The company has grown rapidly by acquiring and revamping last-generation tech brands like AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.

mit_review.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
mit_review.description: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…

mit_review.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
mit_review.description: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is becoming a core operating layer. With its sprawling industrial systems and constant stream of operational…

mit_review.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
mit_review.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s become wildly popular because…

mit_review.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
mit_review.description: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful work when given concise, high-level instructions, and it has access…

mit_review.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
mit_review.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins that leave little room for error. Research shows AI-enabled predictive models can improve crop…
